name: Notice Period Buyout vs. Serve Calculator
h1: Notice Period Buyout vs. Serve Calculator
metaTitle: Notice Period Buyout vs. Serve Calculator — CalcKaro
metaDescription: Calculate whether you should buy out your notice period or serve it. Compare employee buyout costs and company payout differences in India.

faqs.question: What is notice period buyout in India?
faqs.answer: Notice period buyout occurs when an employer or employee pays a sum equivalent to the gross salary of the unserved notice period days to terminate employment immediately.

faqs.question: Is notice period buyout taxable?
faqs.answer: Yes, any notice period buyout amount paid or received is generally taxable as salary under Section 17 of the Income Tax Act in India.

faqs.question: How is notice period buyout calculated?
faqs.answer: It is calculated by multiplying your daily gross salary (monthly gross salary divided by 30 or actual working days) by the number of unserved notice period days.

faqs.question: Can an employer refuse a notice period buyout?
faqs.answer: Yes, unless specified in the employment agreement, an employer has the right to refuse a buyout and request the employee to serve the full notice period.

name: GST + TDS Net Payout Calculator for Freelancers
metaTitle: GST + TDS Net Payout Calculator for Freelancers — CalcKaro
metaDescription: Easily calculate your net take-home earnings as an Indian freelancer after adding GST and deducting TDS (Sec 194J / 194C).

faqs.question: What is the TDS rate for professional services in India?
faqs.answer: Under Section 194J, the TDS rate is 10% for professional fees and technical services. It is 2% under Section 194C for contracts.

faqs.question: Do I have to charge GST as a freelancer in India?
faqs.answer: You must register and charge GST (usually 18% for services) if your annual aggregate turnover exceeds ₹20 Lakhs (₹10 Lakhs for special category states).

faqs.question: Is TDS deducted on the GST component?
faqs.answer: No, according to CBDT circulars, TDS is deducted only on the basic value of the services rendered, excluding the GST component.

faqs.question: Can I claim a refund for deducted TDS?
faqs.answer: Yes, if your final annual tax liability is lower than the total TDS deducted, you can claim a refund by filing your Income Tax Return (ITR).

name: In-hand Salary Calculator (CTC to Take-home)
metaTitle: In-hand Salary Calculator (CTC to Take-home) — CalcKaro
metaDescription: Calculate your monthly in-hand salary from your CTC in India. Computes deductions like EPF, Gratuity, Professional Tax, and Income Tax.

faqs.question: What is the difference between CTC and In-hand salary?
faqs.answer: CTC (Cost to Company) includes all direct and indirect expenses spent on an employee. In-hand salary is the actual amount credited to your bank account after deductions like EPF, Professional Tax, and Income Tax.

faqs.question: Is EPF deduction mandatory?
faqs.answer: Yes, for organizations with 20 or more employees, a deduction of 12% of basic salary towards EPF is mandatory for employees earning up to ₹15,000 per month.

faqs.question: What is Professional Tax in India?
faqs.answer: Professional Tax is a state-level tax levied on salaried individuals. It varies by state but is capped at a maximum of ₹2,500 per financial year.

faqs.question: How is taxable income calculated from CTC?
faqs.answer: Taxable income is calculated by subtracting tax-exempt allowances (like HRA, LTA) and standard deductions from your gross salary.

name: HRA Exemption Calculator
metaDescription: Check your HRA tax exemption under Section 10(13A). Estimate how much tax you save based on your basic salary, rent paid, and city type.

faqs.question: How is HRA tax exemption calculated in India?
faqs.answer: HRA exemption is the minimum of three amounts: (1) Actual HRA received, (2) 50% of basic salary (for metro cities) or 40% (for non-metro cities), or (3) Rent paid minus 10% of basic salary.

faqs.question: Can I claim both HRA and home loan tax benefits?
faqs.answer: Yes, if you live in a rented house and pay rent while also paying home loan EMI for a property located in a different city or not yet occupied.

faqs.question: Do I need a landlord's PAN to claim HRA exemption?
faqs.answer: Yes, providing your landlord's PAN is mandatory if the annual rent paid exceeds ₹1,00,000.

faqs.question: What cities qualify for 50% HRA in India?
faqs.answer: Only four metro cities qualify for the 50% basic salary rule: Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, and Chennai. Other major hubs like Bengaluru or Hyderabad qualify for 40%.

name: Old vs. New Tax Regime Comparison Calculator
h1: Old vs. New Tax Regime Comparison
metaTitle: Old vs. New Tax Regime Comparison Calculator — CalcKaro
metaDescription: Compare your tax liability under the Old and New tax regimes for FY 2025-26 (AY 2026-27). Find the best regime to maximize your savings.

faqs.question: Which tax regime is better: Old or New?
faqs.answer: The New Regime is better if you have few deductions. The Old Regime is usually better if you make substantial tax-saving investments under Section 80C, 80D, and HRA.

faqs.question: What is the standard deduction in the New Tax Regime?
faqs.answer: For salaried individuals, the standard deduction is ₹75,000 under the New Tax Regime (increased from ₹50,000 in recent budgets).

faqs.question: Can I switch between Old and New regimes every year?
faqs.answer: Salaried individuals can choose between the regimes every year when filing ITR, whereas business owners can only switch once in a lifetime.

faqs.question: Is HRA deduction available in the New Tax Regime?
faqs.answer: No, HRA exemption is not available under the New Tax Regime. You must opt for the Old Regime to claim HRA.

name: Gratuity Calculator (Payment of Gratuity Act)
metaTitle: Gratuity Calculator (Payment of Gratuity Act) — CalcKaro
metaDescription: Estimate your gratuity payment in India based on the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972. Inputs: years of service and last drawn basic salary.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for gratuity in India?
faqs.answer: An employee is eligible for gratuity after completing at least 5 years of continuous service with an organization that has 10 or more employees.

faqs.question: What is the formula for calculating gratuity?
faqs.answer: The gratuity formula is: (Last drawn Basic Salary + DA) * (15 / 26) * Number of completed years of service.

faqs.question: Is gratuity taxable in India?
faqs.answer: Gratuity received by government employees is fully tax-exempt. For private-sector employees covered by the Act, it is tax-exempt up to a limit of ₹25 Lakhs.

faqs.question: Can gratuity be paid before 5 years?
faqs.answer: The 5-year requirement is waived only in the unfortunate event of the employee's death or disablement due to an accident or disease.

name: EPF/PF Maturity & Withdrawal Calculator
metaDescription: Calculate your Employee Provident Fund (EPF) maturity amount, interest accumulated, and estimate withdrawal rules and caps.

faqs.question: What is the current EPF interest rate in India?
faqs.answer: The EPF interest rate is determined annually by the EPFO. For recent financial years, the interest rate stands around 8.15% to 8.25%.

faqs.question: How much do the employee and employer contribute to EPF?
faqs.answer: Both contribute 12% of the basic salary. The employee's 12% goes fully to the EPF. The employer's contribution is split: 8.33% to EPS (Pension) and 3.67% to EPF.

faqs.question: Is EPF withdrawal taxable?
faqs.answer: EPF withdrawals are fully tax-free if the employee completes 5 years of continuous service. Withdrawals before 5 years attract TDS unless specific criteria are met.

faqs.question: When can I withdraw my full EPF amount?
faqs.answer: You can withdraw the full amount upon retirement or if you remain unemployed for more than 2 months after leaving a job.

name: Notice Period Salary Calculator
metaTitle: Notice Period Salary Calculator (Pay During Notice) — CalcKaro
metaDescription: Calculate the exact salary payout you will receive during your notice period, adjusting for holidays, unserved days, and local deductions.

faqs.question: How is salary calculated for notice period days?
faqs.answer: Notice period salary is calculated using pro-rata values: (Gross Monthly Salary / Number of days in the month) multiplied by the actual days served.

faqs.question: Do I get full salary during the notice period?
faqs.answer: Yes, you are entitled to your full monthly salary, including basic pay and allowances, for the days you actually serve during your notice period.

faqs.question: Are deductions like EPF still applicable during notice period?
faqs.answer: Yes, statutory deductions like EPF and Professional Tax are deducted from your notice period salary just like regular months.

faqs.question: What happens if I take leaves during the notice period?
faqs.answer: Organization policies differ, but leaves during notice period can extend your last working day or result in loss-of-pay (LOP) salary deductions.

name: Annual Tax Liability Estimator (Form 16 Cross-Check)
metaTitle: Annual Tax Liability Estimator (Form 16 Cross-Check) — CalcKaro
metaDescription: Quickly check your annual tax liability and verify your Form 16 details. Validate deductions, surcharge, cess, and rebates easily.

faqs.question: What is Form 16 in India?
faqs.answer: Form 16 is a certificate issued by your employer detailing the total salary paid to you and the TDS deducted and deposited with the government during the fiscal year.

faqs.question: Why should I cross-check my Form 16?
faqs.answer: Cross-checking helps detect errors in deductions, mismatch in TDS entries (Form 26AS), or verify if all declarations (80C, HRA) were processed correctly.

faqs.question: What is the tax rebate under Section 87A?
faqs.answer: Under Section 87A, individuals with taxable income up to ₹7 Lakhs in the New Regime get a full tax rebate, making their tax liability zero. In the Old Regime, the rebate applies up to ₹5 Lakhs.

faqs.question: What is Health and Education Cess in India?
faqs.answer: It is an additional tax of 4% levied on the total income tax amount calculated before surcharge to support government education and health initiatives.
